title: Power aware arbitration for bus access
abstract: Systems and methods for operating a bus interface unit include queues for receiving and storing one or more words from one or more agents for transmission on to a data bus. From at least a subset of the one or more words, a next word which will cause the least switching power among the subset of the one or more words when transmitted on to the data bus is determined and the next word is selected for transmission on to the data bus, to reduce dynamic power consumption of the data bus. The next word may be selected as a word among the subset of the one or more words with a least Hamming distance from a current word scheduled for transmission on to the data bus.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of operating a bus interface unit, the method comprising: receiving one or more words from one or more agents for transmission on to a data bus; determining, from at least a subset of the one or more words, a next word which will cause the least switching power among the subset of the one or more words when transmitted on to the data bus; and selecting the next word for transmission on to the data bus. 2. The method of claim 1, comprising determining the next word as a word among the subset of the one or more words with a least Hamming distance from a current word scheduled for transmission on to the data bus. 3. The method of claim 2, comprising determining the least Hamming distance based on a fixed quantum scheme or a lowest dynamic work function value for a variable quantum scheme. 4. The method of claim 2, comprising storing the current word scheduled for transmission in a register, prior to transmission on to the data bus. 5. The method of claim 1, comprising storing the one or more words received from the one or more agents in one or more queues, wherein the subset of the one or more words are selected based on a round robin order of the one or more queues. 6. The method of claim 4, comprising maintaining the round robin order with a round robin pointer traversing queues with a pending word in sequential order, and selecting the subset of the one or more words from queues of higher sequential order than a location of the round robin pointer in each of one or more rounds of arbitration. 7. The method of claim 5, comprising masking a queue corresponding to the next word selected for transmission, and not selecting a word from the masked queue until the round robin pointer has traversed a full cycle of all the queues. 8. An apparatus comprising: a data bus; one or more agents; and a bus interface unit configured to: receive one or more words from the one or more agents for transmission on to the data bus; determine, from at least a subset of the one or more words, a next word which will cause the least switching power among the subset of the one or more words when transmitted on to the data bus; and select the next word for transmission on to the data bus. 9. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the bus interface unit comprises: a register configured to store a current word scheduled for transmission on to the data bus; a Hamming compare block configured to determine Hamming distances between each of the subset of the one or more words and the current word, and provide an indication of the next word with the least Hamming distance to the current word; an arbiter configured to receive the indication of the next word and provide a select signal; and a multiplexor configured to select the next word from the subset of the one or more words based on the select signal. 10. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the Hamming compare block is configured to determine the least Hamming distance based on a fixed quantum scheme or a lowest dynamic work function value for a variable quantum scheme. 1 1. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the bus interface unit further comprises one or more queues configured to store the one or more words received from the one or more agents, wherein the arbiter is configured to select the subset of the one or more words from the one or more queues based on a round robin order of the one or more queues. 12. The apparatus of claim 11 , wherein the arbiter is configured to receive an indication of queues which have a pending word. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the arbiter is further configured to maintain the round robin order with a round robin pointer configured to traverse, in sequential order, queues which have pending words, and select the subset of the one or more words from queues of higher sequential order than a location of the round robin pointer in each of one or more rounds of arbitration. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the arbiter is further configured to mask a queue comprising the next word selected for transmission. 15. The apparatus of claim 14, wherein the arbiter is further configured to provide the select signal such that a word from the masked queue is not selected until the round robin pointer has traversed a full cycle of all the queues. 16. The apparatus of claim 8 integrated in at least one semiconductor die. 17. The apparatus of claim 1, integrated in a device selected from the group consisting of a set-top box, a music player, a video player, an entertainment unit, a navigation device, a personal digital assistant (PDA), a fixed location data unit, a server, a computer, a laptop, a tablet, a communications device, and a mobile phone. 18. An apparatus comprising: means for receiving one or more words from one or more agents for transmission on to a data bus; means for determining, from at least a subset of the one or more words, a next word which will cause the least switching power among the subset of the one or more words when transmitted on to the data bus; and means for selecting the next word for transmission on to the data bus. 19. The apparatus of claim 18, comprising means for determining the next word as a word among the subset of the one or more words with a least Hamming distance from a current word scheduled for transmission on to the data bus. 20. The apparatus of claim 19, comprising means for determining the least Hamming distance based on a fixed quantum scheme or a lowest dynamic work function value for a variable quantum scheme. 21. The apparatus of claim 19, comprising means for storing the current word scheduled for transmission, prior to transmission on to the data bus. 22. The apparatus of claim 18, comprising means for storing the one or more words received from the one or more agents, and means for selecting the subset of the one or more words are based on a round robin order of the means for storing the one or more words. 23. The apparatus of claim 22, further comprising means for masking a means for storing the one or more words comprising the next word selected for transmission. 24. A non-transitory computer readable storage medium comprising code, which, when executed by a processor, causes the processor to perform a method of operating a bus interface unit, the non-transitory computer readable storage medium comprising: code for receiving one or more words from one or more agents for transmission on to a data bus; code for determining, from at least a subset of the one or more words, a next word which will cause the least switching power among the subset of the one or more words when transmitted on to the data bus; and code for selecting the next word for transmission on to the data bus. 25. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 24, comprising code for determining the next word as a word among the subset of the one or more words with a least Hamming distance from a current word scheduled for transmission on to the data bus. 26. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 25, comprising code for determining the least Hamming distance based on a fixed quantum scheme or a lowest dynamic work function value for a variable quantum scheme. 27. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 25, comprising code for storing the current word scheduled for transmission in a register, prior to transmission on to the data bus. 28. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 24, comprising code for storing the one or more words received from the one or more agents in one or more queues, wherein the subset of the one or more words are selected based on a round robin order of the one or more queues. 29. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 28, comprising code for maintaining the round robin order with a round robin pointer traversing queues with a pending word in sequential order, and code for selecting the subset of the one or more words from queues of higher sequential order than a location of the round robin pointer in each of one or more rounds of arbitration. 30. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 28, comprising code for masking a queue corresponding to the next word selected for transmission, and code for not selecting a word from the masked queue until the round robin pointer has traversed a full cycle of all the queues.
